The commission is authorized by statute to provide advice to
persons with questions about their responsibilities under the Act.
l,Je do not provide advice to third parties about another person's
duties or obligations unl"ess the third party has been authorized
to request advice by the person whose duties or obligations are in
question. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329. )
In your letter you indicate that your "client is concerned
that Ithe Commission] wi1] be making decj.sions on the matter
without knowtedge of all the facts.'r 
You have not identified who
your client is nor indicated that he or she is a person whose
duti.es and responsibilities under the Act are in question.
In fact, your letter is not a request for advice so much as it j's
one wh j-ch takes issue with the facts and analysis of the advi'ce
request the Commission has received from the Berkeley city
Attorney.

matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.
